title: H2APEX and East Energy Plan Joint Venture Ć¢ā‚¬Å“Hanseatic H2Ć¢ā‚¬Ā¯ for Decentralized Green Hydrogen Projects
content: H2APEX Nova Holding GmbH and East Energy Group have signed a term sheet to establish "Hanseatic H2," a joint venture focused on decentralized green hydrogen projects in Northern and Eastern Germany. The partnership plans to develop four standardized 5MW electrolysis plants to supply RFNBO-compliant hydrogen primarily to the regional mobility sector via trailer deliveries. This initiative allows H2APEX to expand its portfolio to include smaller-scale facilities alongside its major 100MW IPCEI project in Lubmin. East Energy brings expertise in renewable energy project development, while H2APEX contributes construction and operational know-how. By utilizing standardized technical architectures, the venture aims to accelerate planning, reduce costs, and simplify long-term maintenance. The collaboration seeks to advance the regional energy transition and build a reliable, scalable hydrogen infrastructure. The partners intend to promptly establish the project company and begin development.
author: Analysts Account
